body.title: Deactivate goal when pe_chain escalates
body.result: pe_chain deactivates goal on all escalate paths — _close_goal_on_escalate() helper wired into filter_fail, preflight-fail, preflight-timeout, and scope_guard early-return paths. _pe_escalate refactored to use same helper. 3970 tests pass. commit b1cade4a.
body.description:
When pe_chain escalates (preflight fail, scope block, or attempt exhaustion), the active GOAL stays open. goal_continuation re-emits GOAL_READY on the next 2-minute cycle, which fires PROC_CODING_SPRINT again and also lets "LLM territory" (step 4+) run. Igor's main LLM then hallucinates file edits (e.g. core_patterns.py — highest-cited file in system prompts) that are out of scope. scope_guard blocks those edits and posts blank DESIGN PROPOSALs. Fix: pe_chain escalate paths must deactivate the goal (goal_active=False) before returning, so goal_continuation closes it on the next cycle instead of re-firing the chain.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/goal_continuation.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN - deactivating goal on escalate paths when returning early; OUT - changing goal lifecycle otherwise or adding new goal states
**Test plan:** no tests because this is a control-flow fix in pe_chain escalate/return paths (preflight fail, scope block, attempt exhaustion)
